title: Fix silent fallback to dummy results in results_parser.py
description: results_parser.py falls back to dummy/fabricated result data when FRD parsing fails, with no error raised and no warning surfaced to the engineer. An engineer can receive a plausible-looking displacement/stress result that is completely made up. This must raise ResultProcessingError instead of silently returning fake data.

title: Fix geometry fallback to hardcoded [0,0,0]-[10,0,0] in entity_identifier.py
description: When member geometry cannot be parsed from IFC, entity_identifier.py silently substitutes a hardcoded 10m line at the origin. The model then meshes and analyses with completely wrong geometry, producing wrong results with no warning. Should raise ModelExtractionError with the IFC GUID of the failing member.

title: Add CalculiX execution timeout
description: calculix_runner.py has no timeout on the CalculiX subprocess. A diverging analysis or infinite loop hangs the tool indefinitely with no feedback to the engineer. Should add a configurable timeout (default e.g. 300s) via AnalysisConfig, kill the process on expiry, and raise AnalysisError with a clear message.

title: Add pre-analysis model validation
description: Before meshing, the tool should validate that the extracted domain model is well-conditioned: all members have material and section properties, all members are connected to at least one boundary condition or other member (no floating islands), all loads are attached to members, no duplicate member IDs. Currently a disconnected structure is sent to CalculiX and fails obscurely.

title: Fix convergence detection logic bug in output_parser.py
description: Line 129 of output_parser.py uses 'and' instead of 'or': 'if not completed and "ANALYSIS INTERRUPTED" in output_text'. This means a run that has no completion marker AND no INTERRUPTED marker is treated as converged. Should be 'or', so any missing-completion case triggers a non-convergence warning.

title: Fix hardcoded material fallback: missing material data should error, not default to steel
description: When an IFC member has no material or incomplete mechanical properties, properties_extractor.py silently substitutes steel defaults (E=210 GPa, rho=7850 kg/m3). A concrete slab or timber beam then analyses with completely wrong material properties. Should warn loudly (or error) and require explicit material data, not guess.

title: Fix mesh size default inconsistency: CLI help says 1.0 m, code defaults to 0.1 m
description: The --mesh-size option in commands.py advertises a default of 1.0 m in the help text, but the actual default in the code is 0.1 m. This is confusing and produces unexpectedly fine (slow) meshes without the engineer requesting them. Pick one value, document it consistently, and use it everywhere.

title: Fix multiple load cases: each IfcStructuralLoadCase should map to a separate CalculiX STEP
description: Models with multiple IfcStructuralLoadCase entries (Dead, Live, Wind, Earthquake etc.) currently only get a subset of cases written to the INP. Each load case should produce a separate *STEP in the CalculiX input file so all cases are analysed and their results are tagged by case name. This affects correctness for any real multi-case building model.

title: Improve self-weight / gravity load handling
description: Models without explicit IfcStructuralLoadCase entries rely entirely on self-weight, but the --gravity flag must be passed manually. The library has no way to auto-detect that gravity is the intended load. Consider smarter defaults or detection.

title: Validate linear buckling analysis against published benchmarks
description: The linear_buckling analysis type produces two CalculiX steps (static pre-stress + perturbation buckle) and parses eigenvalue multipliers from the .dat file, but results have not been validated against any published benchmark. Add a validation test.

title: Write missing unit tests for recent changes
description: Several recent bug fixes and features lack unit test coverage: _validate_model(), loads_extractor GRAVITY group fix, LoadConfiguration averaging, L/T/C section extractors, non-SI unit warning, CLI --timeout.

title: Fix IfcStructuralLoadConfiguration: only first load value is used
description: loads_extractor.py line 675-679 reads IfcStructuralLoadConfiguration but only uses the first value from the Values array. Multi-point load distributions (e.g. trapezoidal line loads) are silently truncated to a point load. Should either implement proper interpolated distribution or raise a clear not-implemented warning.

title: Document and stabilise result JSON schema for downstream tools
description: The results JSON written by run_enhanced_analysis() has no documented schema. Keys, units, and structure can change between versions without warning, breaking downstream tools that process the results. Define a versioned schema (e.g. results_v1.json), document it, and add a schema version key to the output. Units should be explicitly stated (SI: metres, Newtons, Pascals).

title: Consolidate duplicate error/convergence detection logic
description: OutputParser (output_parser.py) and ErrorDetector (error_handling.py) independently define the same ~40 CalculiX error patterns and convergence checks. Any fix or new pattern must be applied in two places. Consolidate into a single implementation — OutputParser should delegate to ErrorDetector, or merge into one class.

title: Validate IFC project units and warn on non-SI input
description: The extractor applies unit scale factors but never validates that they are reasonable. A model authored in imperial units (feet, kips) with a missing unit declaration will produce silently wrong results. Should check extracted unit scales against expected SI ranges and warn/error if they look wrong (e.g. length_scale not in [0.001, 1000]).

title: Support I-section beam profiles natively
description: Only rectangular, circular, pipe (hollow circle), and box (hollow rectangle) cross-sections are supported for CalculiX B31 beam elements. I-sections are currently approximated as equivalent rectangles preserving area and second moment of area. Proper CalculiX B31 I-section support should be added.

title: Support partial connection end-releases
description: Connection end-releases read from IfcRelConnectsStructuralMember.AppliedCondition are modelled as full pins (all three rotational DOFs released). Partial releases — where only one member or one rotation axis is released — are not yet supported.

title: Fix overlapping members losing physical group during mesh fragmentation
description: Members with identical or overlapping geometry may lose their physical group assignment during fragment(). Their sections are skipped with a warning; a fallback assigns the nearest elements by spatial proximity. Need a robust fix so physical groups survive fragmentation.

title: Fix connection geometry: use IFC topology instead of proximity search
description: Currently connections are resolved by geometric proximity (0.5 m tolerance) rather than using the IfcRelConnectsStructuralMember relationship topology defined in the IFC file. This can cause incorrect connectivity for closely spaced but unconnected members.

title: Fix mixed beam+shell instability: rotational zero-energy modes under lateral load
description: When B31 beam elements and shell elements are connected only via DOF 1-3 EQUATION constraints, and column bases are pinned (DOF 1-3 only from IfcBoundaryNodeCondition), the structure has rotational zero-energy modes under lateral load. building_01 demonstrates this: lateral forces on a pinned-base frame with no moment-resisting connections produce a near-mechanism. At minimum, the library should detect and warn about this condition rather than silently producing wrong results. The fix may involve upgrading shared beam+shell node connections to DOF 1-6 EQUATION constraints where appropriate.

title: Fix intermediate support positions being silently omitted
description: If a support connection is at an intermediate point on a beam (not at a beam endpoint), Gmsh may not place a mesh node at that exact location, causing the support to be silently omitted. The grid_of_beams example demonstrates this.
